en.content: The value of the integral $\int\limits_0^2 \frac{\sqrt{x\left(x^2+x+1\right)}}{(\sqrt{x+1})\left(\sqrt{x^4+x^2+1}\right)} \mathrm{d} x$ is equal to:
en.explanation:
$I=\int\limits_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x\left(x^2+x+1\right)}}{(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^4+x^2+1}\right)}\right] d x$

$\begin{aligned} & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x\left(x^2+x+1\right)}}{(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^4+2 x^2+1-x^2}\right)}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x\left(x^2+x+1\right)}}{(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{\left(x^2+1\right)^2-x^2}\right)}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x\left(x^2+x+1\right)}}{\left.(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left\{\sqrt{\left(x^2+1+x\right)\left(x^2+1-x\right)}\right\}\right]}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{(\sqrt{x}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^2+x+1}\right)}{(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^2+1+x}\right) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^2+1-x}\right)}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x}}{(\sqrt{x+1}) \cdot\left(\sqrt{x^2-x+1}\right)}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{(x+1)\left(x^2-x+1\right)}}\right] d x \\ & =\int_0^2\left[\frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x^3+1}}\right] d x\end{aligned}$

Let $x=t^2 \Rightarrow d x=2 t d t$

$\boldsymbol{I}=\int_0^{\sqrt{2}}\left[\frac{2 t^2}{\sqrt{t^6+1}}\right] d t=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) \int_0^{\sqrt{2}}\left[\frac{3 t^2}{\sqrt{t^6+1}}\right] d t$

Let $t^3=u \Rightarrow 3 t^2 d t=d u$

$\boldsymbol{I}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 \cdot \int_0^{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\frac{\boldsymbol{d u}}{\sqrt{u^2+1}}\right]$

Using standard integration formulaes :

$\int\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x^2+a^2}}\right) \boldsymbol{d x}=\ln \left|\boldsymbol{x}+\sqrt{\boldsymbol{x}^2+\boldsymbol{a}^2}\right|+C$

= $\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left|\boldsymbol{u}+\sqrt{\boldsymbol{u}^2+1}\right|\right]_0^{2 \sqrt{2}}$

$=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) \cdot[\ln (2 \sqrt{2}+3)-\ln 1]=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) \cdot[\ln (3+2 \sqrt{2})]$

मानक समाकलन सूत्रों का उपयोग करने पर :

en.content: Let $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}\frac{1}{3}, & x \leq \pi / 2 \\ \frac{\mathrm{~b}(1-\sin x)}{(\pi-2 x)^2}, & x>\pi / 2\end{array}\right.$. If $f$ is continuous at $x=\pi / 2$, then the value of $\int\limits_0^{3 \mathrm{~b}-6}\left|x^2+2 x-3\right| \mathrm{d} x$ is :
en.explanation:
The function $\boldsymbol{f}(\boldsymbol{x})$ is continuous at $\boldsymbol{x}=\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}$ where

$\boldsymbol{f}(\boldsymbol{x})=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{1}{3} & ; x \leqslant \frac{\pi}{2} \\ \frac{b(1-\sin x)}{(\boldsymbol{\pi}-2 x)^2} & ; x>\frac{\pi}{2}\end{array} \cdots(i)\right.$.

We observe the LHL from equation $(i)$ :

$$ \lim\limits_{x \rightarrow\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)^{-}} f(x)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{1}{3} \ldots \ldots(i i) $$

We must have the RHL from equation (i) to be $\frac{1}{3}$ for the function $\boldsymbol{f}(\boldsymbol{x})$ to be continuous at $\boldsymbol{x}=\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}$ :

$$ \lim\limits_{\boldsymbol{x} \rightarrow\left(\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}\right)^{+}} \boldsymbol{f ( x )}=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow \lim\limits _{\boldsymbol{x} \rightarrow\left(\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}\right)^{+}} \frac{\boldsymbol{b}(1-\sin \boldsymbol{x})}{(\boldsymbol{\pi}-2 x)^2}=\frac{1}{3} \ldots \ldots(\boldsymbol{i i i}) $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow\left(\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}\right)^{+}} \frac{\boldsymbol{b}(1-\sin \boldsymbol{x})}{(\boldsymbol{\pi}-2 \boldsymbol{x})^2}=\frac{0}{0}$ format $\leftarrow$ Apply L'hôpital's rule

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow\left(\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{2}\right)^{+}} \frac{b \cos \boldsymbol{x}}{4(\boldsymbol{\pi}-2 \boldsymbol{x})}=\frac{0}{0}$ format ค Apply L'hôpital's rule once more

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)^{+}} \frac{b \sin x}{8}=\frac{b}{8} \ldots \ldots(i v)$

We can mathematically demonstrate conditional continuity of $f(x)$ at $x=\frac{\pi}{2}$ from equation (iii) and (iv) :

$$ \frac{b}{8}=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow b=\frac{8}{3} \ldots \ldots(v) $$

Now, I = $\int\limits_0^{3 \mathrm{~b}-6}\left|x^2+2 x-3\right| \mathrm{d} x$

$=\int\limits_0^{(3)\left(\frac{8}{3}\right)-6}\left|\boldsymbol{x}^2+2 \boldsymbol{x}-3\right| \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{x}$

$=\int\limits_0^2\left|x^2+2 x-3\right| d x$

$=\int\limits_0^2|(x+3)(x-1)| d x$

$\left\{\begin{array}{l}(x+3)(x-1) \geqslant 0 ; x \in(-\infty,-3] \\ (x+3)(x-1) \leqslant 0 ; x \in[-3,1] \\ (x+3)(x-1) \geqslant 0 ; x \in[1, \infty)\end{array}\right.$

$=\int\limits_0^1(x+3)(1-x) d x$ $+\int\limits_1^2(x+3)(x-1) d x$

$=\int\limits_0^1\left(3-2 x-x^2\right) d x$ $+\int\limits_1^2\left(x^2+2 x-3\right) d x$

$\begin{aligned} & =\left[3 x-x^2-\frac{x^3}{3}\right]_0^1 +\left[\frac{x^3}{3}+x^2-3 x\right]_1^2\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} & =\left(3-1-\frac{1}{3}\right) +\left(\frac{8}{3}+4-6\right)-\left(\frac{1}{3}+1-3\right)\end{aligned}$

= 4

en.content: If $\int\limits_{\pi / 6}^{\pi / 4}\left(\cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cot \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+1\right) d x=\alpha \log _{\mathrm{e}}(\sqrt{3}-1)$, then $9 \alpha^2$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
$\boldsymbol{I}=\int\limits_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\cot \left(\boldsymbol{x}-\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{3}\right) \cdot \cot \left(\boldsymbol{x}+\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{3}\right)+1\right] \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{x}$

$\begin{aligned} & =\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left|\frac{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right| d x \\ & =\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left|\frac{\cos \left\{\left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)-\left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right| d x \\ & =\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right] d x\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{2 \sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right] d x$

$\begin{aligned} & =2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\left\{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}-\left\{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)-\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right]} d x\right. \\ & =2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)-\cos (2 x)}\right] d x\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=-\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{-\frac{1}{2}-\cos (2 x)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{1+2 \cos (2 x)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{1+2\left(1-2 \sin ^2 x\right)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{\left.3-4 \sin ^2 x\right)}\right] d x$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3 \sec ^2 x-4 \tan ^2 x}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)-4 \tan ^2 x}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3-\tan ^2 x}\right] d x$

Substitute $\boldsymbol{t}=\tan \boldsymbol{x} \Rightarrow \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{t}=\left(\sec ^2 \boldsymbol{x}\right) \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{x}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{2}{3-t^2}\right] d t=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{2 \sqrt{3}}{(\sqrt{3}+t)(\sqrt{3}-t)}\right] d t$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{(\sqrt{3}+t)+(\sqrt{3}-t)}{(\sqrt{3}+t)(\sqrt{3}-t)}\right] d t=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}-t}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+t}\right] d t$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot[\ln (\sqrt{3}+t)-\ln (\sqrt{3}-t)]_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{3}+t}{\sqrt{3}-t}\right)\right]_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{3}+1}{\sqrt{3}-1}\right)-\ln 2\right]=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left\{\frac{2}{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}-\ln 2\right]$

$\boldsymbol{I}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{\frac{2}{2(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{\frac{1}{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{(\sqrt{3}-1)^{-2}\right\}=\left(-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln (\sqrt{3}-1) \ldots \ldots(i)$

$$\therefore $$ $ \alpha=-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \Rightarrow 9 \alpha^2=12$

hi.content: $$ \text { यदि } \int\limits_{\pi / 6}^{\pi / 4}\left(\cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cot \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+1\right) d x=\alpha \log _{\mathrm{e}}(\sqrt{3}-1) \text { है, तो } 9 \alpha^2 \text { बराबर है } $$
hi.explanation:
$\boldsymbol{I}=\int\limits_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\cot \left(\boldsymbol{x}-\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{3}\right) \cdot \cot \left(\boldsymbol{x}+\frac{\boldsymbol{\pi}}{3}\right)+1\right] \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{x}$

$\begin{aligned} & =\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left|\frac{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right| d x \\ & =\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left|\frac{\cos \left\{\left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)-\left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right| d x \\ & =\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right] d x\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{2 \sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}\right] d x$

$\begin{aligned} & =2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\left\{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}-\left\{\cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)-\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cdot \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}} d x\right. \\ & =2 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)-\cos (2 x)}\right] d x\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=-\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{1}{-\frac{1}{2}-\cos (2 x)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{1+2 \cos (2 x)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{1+2\left(1-2 \sin ^2 x\right)}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2}{\left.3-4 \sin ^2 x\right)}\right] d x$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3 \sec ^2 x-4 \tan ^2 x}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)-4 \tan ^2 x}\right] d x=\int_{\frac{\pi}{6}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left[\frac{2 \sec ^2 x}{3-\tan ^2 x}\right] d x$

$\boldsymbol{t}=\tan \boldsymbol{x}$ प्रतिस्थापित करने पर $\Rightarrow \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{t}=\left(\sec ^2 \boldsymbol{x}\right) \boldsymbol{d} \boldsymbol{x}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{2}{3-t^2}\right] d t=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{2 \sqrt{3}}{(\sqrt{3}+t)(\sqrt{3}-t)}\right] d t$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{(\sqrt{3}+t)+(\sqrt{3}-t)}{(\sqrt{3}+t)(\sqrt{3}-t)}\right] d t=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \int_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}-t}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+t}\right] d t$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\boldsymbol{I}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot[\ln (\sqrt{3}+t)-\ln (\sqrt{3}-t)]_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{3}+t}{\sqrt{3}-t}\right)\right]_{\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}^1$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{3}+1}{\sqrt{3}-1}\right)-\ln 2\right]=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot\left[\ln \left\{\frac{2}{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}-\ln 2\right]$

$\boldsymbol{I}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{\frac{2}{2(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{\frac{1}{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}\right\}$

$=\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln \left\{(\sqrt{3}-1)^{-2}\right\}=\left(-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \cdot \ln (\sqrt{3}-1) \ldots \ldots(i)$

$$\therefore $$ $ \alpha=-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \Rightarrow 9 \alpha^2=12$

en.content: Let $\mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & -1 \\ 2 & 1 & \alpha \\ 0 & 1 & -1\end{array}\right]$ be a singular matrix. Let $f(x)=\int_0^x\left(\mathrm{t}^2+2 \mathrm{t}+3\right) \mathrm{dt}, x \in[1, \alpha]$. If M and m are respectively the maximum and the minimum values of $f$ in $[1, \alpha]$, then $3(\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{m})$ is equal to :
en.explanation:
Since $A$ is a singular matrix, its determinant must be zero.

We have

$$ A=\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 3 & -1\\ 2 & 1 & \alpha\\ 0 & 1 & -1 \end{bmatrix} $$

So,

$$ |A|=0 $$

Now let us calculate the determinant by expanding along the first row:

$$ |A|=1\begin{vmatrix}1 & \alpha\\ 1 & -1\end{vmatrix} -3\begin{vmatrix}2 & \alpha\\ 0 & -1\end{vmatrix} +(-1)\begin{vmatrix}2 & 1\\ 0 & 1\end{vmatrix} $$

$$ =1[(1)(-1)-(\alpha)(1)]-3[(2)(-1)-(\alpha)(0)]-1[(2)(1)-(1)(0)] $$

$$ =(-1-\alpha)-3(-2)-2 $$

$$ =-1-\alpha+6-2 $$

$$ =3-\alpha $$

Since $|A|=0$,

$$ 3-\alpha=0 \Rightarrow \alpha=3 $$

Now,

$$ f(x)=\int_0^x (t^2+2t+3)\,dt $$

Integrating,

$$ f(x)=\left[\frac{t^3}{3}+t^2+3t\right]_0^x =\frac{x^3}{3}+x^2+3x $$

We need maximum and minimum values of $f(x)$ in $[1,\alpha]=[1,3]$.

Now check whether $f(x)$ is increasing or decreasing.

$$ f'(x)=x^2+2x+3 $$

$$ f'(x)=x^2+2x+3=(x+1)^2+2>0 \quad \text{for all } x $$

So $f(x)$ is strictly increasing on $[1,3]$.

Hence,

  • minimum value at $x=1$

  • maximum value at $x=3$

Now,

$$ m=f(1)=\frac{1}{3}+1+3=\frac{13}{3} $$

and

$$ M=f(3)=\frac{27}{3}+9+9=9+9+9=27 $$

Therefore,

$$ M-m=27-\frac{13}{3} =\frac{81-13}{3} =\frac{68}{3} $$

So,

$$ 3(M-m)=3\cdot \frac{68}{3}=68 $$

Hence the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{68} $$

So, the correct option is $\boxed{\text{Option B}}$.

hi.content: माना $\mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & -1 \\ 2 & 1 & \alpha \\ 0 & 1 & -1\end{array}\right]$ एक अव्युत्क्रमणीय आव्यूह है। माना $f(x)=\int_0^x\left(\mathrm{t}^2+2 \mathrm{t}+3\right) \mathrm{dt}, x \in[1, \alpha]$ है। यदि $[1, \alpha]$ में, $f$ के अधिकतम और निम्नतम मान क्रमश: M और m हैं, तो $3(\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{m})$ बराबर है :
hi.explanation:
चूँकि $A$ एक अव्युत्क्रमणीय आव्यूह (singular matrix) है, इसलिए इसका सारणिक शून्य होना चाहिए।

हमारे पास है,

$$ A=\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 3 & -1\\ 2 & 1 & \alpha\\ 0 & 1 & -1 \end{bmatrix} $$

इसलिए,

$$ |A|=0 $$

अब पहली पंक्ति के अनुदिश प्रसार करके सारणिक की गणना करते हैं:

$$ |A|=1\begin{vmatrix}1 & \alpha\\ 1 & -1\end{vmatrix} -3\begin{vmatrix}2 & \alpha\\ 0 & -1\end{vmatrix} +(-1)\begin{vmatrix}2 & 1\\ 0 & 1\end{vmatrix} $$

$$ =1[(1)(-1)-(\alpha)(1)]-3[(2)(-1)-(\alpha)(0)]-1[(2)(1)-(1)(0)] $$

$$ =(-1-\alpha)-3(-2)-2 $$

$$ =-1-\alpha+6-2 $$

$$ =3-\alpha $$

चूँकि $|A|=0$,

$$ 3-\alpha=0 \Rightarrow \alpha=3 $$

अब,

$$ f(x)=\int_0^x (t^2+2t+3)\,dt $$

समाकलन करने पर,

$$ f(x)=\left[\frac{t^3}{3}+t^2+3t\right]_0^x =\frac{x^3}{3}+x^2+3x $$

हमें $[1,\alpha]=[1,3]$ में $f(x)$ के अधिकतम और न्यूनतम मानों की आवश्यकता है।

अब जाँच करते हैं कि $f(x)$ वर्धमान है या ह्रासमान।

$$ f'(x)=x^2+2x+3 $$

$$ f'(x)=x^2+2x+3=(x+1)^2+2>0 \quad \text{सभी } x \text{ के लिए} $$

इसलिए $f(x)$, $[1,3]$ पर निरंतर वर्धमान है।

अत:,

  • $x=1$ पर न्यूनतम मान

  • $x=3$ पर अधिकतम मान

अब,

$$ m=f(1)=\frac{1}{3}+1+3=\frac{13}{3} $$

और

$$ M=f(3)=\frac{27}{3}+9+9=9+9+9=27 $$

इसलिए,

$$ M-m=27-\frac{13}{3} =\frac{81-13}{3} =\frac{68}{3} $$

अतः,

$$ 3(M-m)=3\cdot \frac{68}{3}=68 $$

अतः सही उत्तर है:

$$ \boxed{68} $$

इसलिए, सही विकल्प $\boxed{\text{विकल्प B}}$ है।

en.content: The value of the integral $\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{32 \cos ^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}}\right) d x$ is :
en.explanation:
Let

$$ I=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}}\,dx $$

We will use the standard property:

If

$$ I=\int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx, $$

then

$$ I=\int_{-a}^{a} f(-x)\,dx $$

and hence

$$ 2I=\int_{-a}^{a} \bigl(f(x)+f(-x)\bigr)\,dx $$

Now here,

$$ f(x)=\frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}} $$

So,

$$ f(-x)=\frac{32\cos^4(-x)}{1+e^{\sin(-x)}} $$

Using $\cos(-x)=\cos x$ and $\sin(-x)=-\sin x$,

$$ f(-x)=\frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{-\sin x}} $$

Therefore,

$$ f(x)+f(-x) = 32\cos^4 x\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}}\right) $$

Now simplify the bracket:

$$ \frac{1}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}} $$

Let $t=e^{\sin x}$. Then $e^{-\sin x}=\frac{1}{t}$. So,

$$ \frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{t}} = \frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{t}{1+t} = 1 $$

Hence,

$$ f(x)+f(-x)=32\cos^4 x $$

So,

$$ 2I=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} 32\cos^4 x\,dx $$

Thus,

$$ I=16\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \cos^4 x\,dx $$

Since $\cos^4 x$ is an even function,

$$ I=32\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \cos^4 x\,dx $$

Now use the identity

$$ \cos^4 x=\left(\cos^2 x\right)^2 =\left(\frac{1+\cos 2x}{2}\right)^2 $$

which gives

$$ \cos^4 x=\frac{3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x}{8} $$

Therefore,

$$ I=32\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x}{8}\,dx $$

$$ I=4\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} (3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x)\,dx $$

Now integrate termwise:

$$ \int 3\,dx=3x,\qquad \int 4\cos 2x\,dx=2\sin 2x,\qquad \int \cos 4x\,dx=\frac{\sin 4x}{4} $$

So,

$$ I=4\left[3x+2\sin 2x+\frac{\sin 4x}{4}\right]_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} $$

At $x=\frac{\pi}{4}$,

$$ 3x=\frac{3\pi}{4},\qquad \sin 2x=\sin \frac{\pi}{2}=1,\qquad \sin 4x=\sin \pi=0 $$

At $x=0$,

$$ 3x=0,\qquad \sin 0=0 $$

Hence,

$$ I=4\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}+2\right) $$

$$ I=3\pi+8 $$

So the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{3\pi+8} $$

Hence, Option B is correct.

hi.content: समाकलन $\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{32 \cos ^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}}\right) d x$ का मान है:
hi.explanation:
माना

$$ I=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}}\,dx $$

हम मानक गुणधर्म का उपयोग करेंगे:

यदि

$$ I=\int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx, $$

तब

$$ I=\int_{-a}^{a} f(-x)\,dx $$

और इसलिए

$$ 2I=\int_{-a}^{a} \bigl(f(x)+f(-x)\bigr)\,dx $$

अब यहाँ,

$$ f(x)=\frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{\sin x}} $$

इसलिए,

$$ f(-x)=\frac{32\cos^4(-x)}{1+e^{\sin(-x)}} $$

$\cos(-x)=\cos x$ और $\sin(-x)=-\sin x$ का उपयोग करने पर,

$$ f(-x)=\frac{32\cos^4 x}{1+e^{-\sin x}} $$

इसलिए,

$$ f(x)+f(-x) = 32\cos^4 x\left(\frac{1}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}}\right) $$

अब कोष्ठक को सरल करते हैं:

$$ \frac{1}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}} $$

माना $t=e^{\sin x}$। तब $e^{-\sin x}=\frac{1}{t}$। इसलिए,

$$ \frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{1}{1+\frac{1}{t}} = \frac{1}{1+t}+\frac{t}{1+t} = 1 $$

अतः,

$$ f(x)+f(-x)=32\cos^4 x $$

इसलिए,

$$ 2I=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} 32\cos^4 x\,dx $$

इस प्रकार,

$$ I=16\int_{-\frac{\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \cos^4 x\,dx $$

चूँकि $\cos^4 x$ एक सम फलन (even function) है,

$$ I=32\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \cos^4 x\,dx $$

अब सर्वसमिका का उपयोग करते हैं

$$ \cos^4 x=\left(\cos^2 x\right)^2 =\left(\frac{1+\cos 2x}{2}\right)^2 $$

जिससे प्राप्त होता है

$$ \cos^4 x=\frac{3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x}{8} $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=32\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x}{8}\,dx $$

$$ I=4\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} (3+4\cos 2x+\cos 4x)\,dx $$

अब पदवार समाकलन करने पर:

$$ \int 3\,dx=3x,\qquad \int 4\cos 2x\,dx=2\sin 2x,\qquad \int \cos 4x\,dx=\frac{\sin 4x}{4} $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=4\left[3x+2\sin 2x+\frac{\sin 4x}{4}\right]_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} $$

$x=\frac{\pi}{4}$ पर,

$$ 3x=\frac{3\pi}{4},\qquad \sin 2x=\sin \frac{\pi}{2}=1,\qquad \sin 4x=\sin \pi=0 $$

$x=0$ पर,

$$ 3x=0,\qquad \sin 0=0 $$

अतः,

$$ I=4\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}+2\right) $$

$$ I=3\pi+8 $$

अतः सही उत्तर है:

$$ \boxed{3\pi+8} $$

अतः, विकल्प B सही है।

$\begin{aligned} & \text { Put } x=2 t \Rightarrow d x=2 d t \\ & I=\int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ell n 2 t}{4 t^2+4}(2 d t)=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ell n 2+\ell n t}{t^2+1} d t \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ell n 2}{t^2+1} d t+\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ell n t}{t^2+1} d t\end{aligned}$

= $\left[\frac{\ln 2}{2} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right]_0^{\infty}+\mathrm{I}_1$

$=\frac{\ell \mathrm{n} 2}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}+\mathrm{I}_1$

$\mathrm{I}_2=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ell \mathrm{nt}}{\mathrm{t}^2+1} \mathrm{dt}$

$t=\frac{1}{u} \Rightarrow d t=-\frac{d u}{u^2}$

$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2} \int_{\infty}^0 \frac{\ell \mathrm{n}(1 / \mathrm{u})}{\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}^2}+1}\left(-\frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{u}^2}\right) \\ & \operatorname{Add} \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_1=0 \\ & \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi \ell \mathrm{n} 2}{4}\end{aligned}$

en.content: The value of the integral $\int\limits_0^{\infty} \frac{\log _e(x)}{x^2+4} d x$ is:
en.explanation:
Let

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln x}{x^2+4}\,dx $$

We will use a standard substitution, as done in NCERT-type definite integral problems.

Take

$$ x=2t $$

Then

$$ dx=2\,dt $$

and when $x=0,\ t=0$, and when $x\to\infty,\ t\to\infty$.

So,

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{(2t)^2+4}\cdot 2\,dt $$

Simplify the denominator:

$$ (2t)^2+4=4t^2+4=4(1+t^2) $$

Hence,

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{4(1+t^2)}\cdot 2\,dt =\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

Now use

$$ \ln(2t)=\ln 2+\ln t $$

So,

$$ I=\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln 2}{1+t^2}\,dt+\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

That is,

$$ I=\frac{\ln 2}{2}\int_0^{\infty}\frac{dt}{1+t^2}+\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

Now,

$$ \int_0^{\infty}\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1} t\right]_0^{\infty}=\frac{\pi}{2} $$

Also, let

$$ J=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

Using the substitution $t=\frac1u$, we get

$$ dt=-\frac{1}{u^2}\,du $$

Then

$$ J=\int_{\infty}^{0}\frac{\ln(1/u)}{1+1/u^2}\left(-\frac{1}{u^2}\right)du $$

This simplifies to

$$ J=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{-\ln u}{1+u^2}\,du =-J $$

Therefore,

$$ J=0 $$

So,

$$ I=\frac{\ln 2}{2}\cdot \frac{\pi}{2} =\frac{\pi\ln 2}{4} $$

Hence, the value of the integral is

$$ \boxed{\frac{\pi\ln 2}{4}} $$

So, the correct option is:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option B}} $$

hi.content: $$ \text { समाकलन } \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\log _e(x)}{x^2+4} d x \text { का मान है: } $$
hi.explanation:
माना

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln x}{x^2+4}\,dx $$

हम एक मानक प्रतिस्थापन का उपयोग करेंगे, जैसा कि NCERT प्रकार की निश्चित समाकलन समस्याओं में किया जाता है।

माना

$$ x=2t $$

तब

$$ dx=2\,dt $$

और जब $x=0,\ t=0$, तथा जब $x\to\infty,\ t\to\infty$ है।

इसलिए,

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{(2t)^2+4}\cdot 2\,dt $$

हर को सरल करने पर:

$$ (2t)^2+4=4t^2+4=4(1+t^2) $$

अतः,

$$ I=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{4(1+t^2)}\cdot 2\,dt =\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln(2t)}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

अब, निम्नलिखित का उपयोग करें:

$$ \ln(2t)=\ln 2+\ln t $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln 2}{1+t^2}\,dt+\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

अर्थात्,

$$ I=\frac{\ln 2}{2}\int_0^{\infty}\frac{dt}{1+t^2}+\frac12\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

अब,

$$ \int_0^{\infty}\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1} t\right]_0^{\infty}=\frac{\pi}{2} $$

साथ ही, माना

$$ J=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{\ln t}{1+t^2}\,dt $$

प्रतिस्थापन $t=\frac1u$ का उपयोग करने पर, हमें प्राप्त होता है:

$$ dt=-\frac{1}{u^2}\,du $$

तब

$$ J=\int_{\infty}^{0}\frac{\ln(1/u)}{1+1/u^2}\left(-\frac{1}{u^2}\right)du $$

इसे सरल करने पर प्राप्त होता है:

$$ J=\int_0^{\infty}\frac{-\ln u}{1+u^2}\,du =-J $$

इसलिए,

$$ J=0 $$

अतः,

$$ I=\frac{\ln 2}{2}\cdot \frac{\pi}{2} =\frac{\pi\ln 2}{4} $$

अतः, समाकलन का मान है:

$$ \boxed{\frac{\pi\ln 2}{4}} $$

इसलिए, सही विकल्प है:

$$ \boxed{\text{विकल्प B}} $$

en.content: The integral $\int\limits_0^1 \cot ^{-1}\left(1+x+x^2\right) d x$ is equal to :
en.explanation:
Let

$$ I=\int_0^1 \cot^{-1}(1+x+x^2)\,dx $$

We will use the standard identity

$$ \cot^{-1} t=\tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{t}\right)\quad \text{for } t>0 $$

Since $1+x+x^2>0$ on $[0,1]$, we can write

$$ I=\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right)\,dx $$

Now use the formula

$$ \tan^{-1} a-\tan^{-1} b=\tan^{-1}\left(\frac{a-b}{1+ab}\right) $$

We try to express

$$ \frac{1}{1+x+x^2} $$

in a suitable form. Observe that

$$ \tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{(x+1)-x}{1+x(x+1)}\right) = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right) $$

Hence,

$$ \cot^{-1}(1+x+x^2)=\tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x $$

So,

$$ I=\int_0^1 \left[\tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x\right]dx $$

Therefore,

$$ I=\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(x+1)\,dx-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx $$

In the first integral, put $u=x+1$. Then when $x=0$, $u=1$, and when $x=1$, $u=2$. So

$$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(x+1)\,dx=\int_1^2 \tan^{-1}u\,du $$

Thus,

$$ I=\int_1^2 \tan^{-1}u\,du-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \int_1^2 \tan^{-1}x\,dx-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx $$

Now use

$$ \int \tan^{-1}x\,dx=x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2) $$

So,

$$ \int_1^2 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_1^2 $$

and

$$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_0^1 $$

Hence,

$$ I= \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_1^2 - \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_0^1 $$

This becomes

$$ I= \left(2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5\right) - \left(\tan^{-1}1-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) -\left(\tan^{-1}1-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) $$

since at $x=0$, the value is $0$.

Now $\tan^{-1}1=\dfrac{\pi}{4}$, so

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5-2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) $$

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5-\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2 $$

Now combine the logarithms:

$$ \log 2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5 = \frac{1}{2}\log 4-\frac{1}{2}\log 5 = \frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{4}{5}\right) = -\frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{5}{4}\right) $$

Therefore,

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)-\frac{\pi}{2} $$

So the correct answer is

$$ \boxed{2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log_e\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)-\frac{\pi}{2}} $$

Hence, the correct option is:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option D}} $$

hi.content: $$ \text { समाकलन } \int_0^1 \cot ^{-1}\left(1+x+x^2\right) d x \text { बराबर है: } $$
hi.explanation:
माना

$$ I=\int_0^1 \cot^{-1}(1+x+x^2)\,dx $$

हम मानक सर्वसमिका का उपयोग करेंगे

$$ \cot^{-1} t=\tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{t}\right)\quad \text{for } t>0 $$

चूँकि $[0,1]$ पर $1+x+x^2>0$ है, इसलिए हम लिख सकते हैं

$$ I=\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right)\,dx $$

अब सूत्र का उपयोग करें

$$ \tan^{-1} a-\tan^{-1} b=\tan^{-1}\left(\frac{a-b}{1+ab}\right) $$

हम

$$ \frac{1}{1+x+x^2} $$

को एक उपयुक्त रूप में व्यक्त करने का प्रयास करते हैं। ध्यान दें कि

$$ \tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{(x+1)-x}{1+x(x+1)}\right) = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{1+x+x^2}\right) $$

अतः,

$$ \cot^{-1}(1+x+x^2)=\tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=\int_0^1 \left[\tan^{-1}(x+1)-\tan^{-1}x\right]dx $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(x+1)\,dx-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx $$

पहले समाकलन में, $u=x+1$ रखें। तब जब $x=0$, $u=1$, और जब $x=1$, $u=2$ होगा। इसलिए

$$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(x+1)\,dx=\int_1^2 \tan^{-1}u\,du $$

इस प्रकार,

$$ I=\int_1^2 \tan^{-1}u\,du-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \int_1^2 \tan^{-1}x\,dx-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx $$

अब निम्न का उपयोग करें

$$ \int \tan^{-1}x\,dx=x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2) $$

इसलिए,

$$ \int_1^2 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_1^2 $$

और

$$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}x\,dx = \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_0^1 $$

अतः,

$$ I= \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_1^2 - \left[x\tan^{-1}x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1+x^2)\right]_0^1 $$

यह बन जाता है

$$ I= \left(2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5\right) - \left(\tan^{-1}1-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) -\left(\tan^{-1}1-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) $$

चूँकि $x=0$ पर, इसका मान $0$ है।

अब $\tan^{-1}1=\dfrac{\pi}{4}$, इसलिए

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5-2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2}\log 2\right) $$

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5-\frac{\pi}{2}+\log 2 $$

अब लघुगणक पदों को संयोजित करें:

$$ \log 2-\frac{1}{2}\log 5 = \frac{1}{2}\log 4-\frac{1}{2}\log 5 = \frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{4}{5}\right) = -\frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{5}{4}\right) $$

इसलिए,

$$ I=2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)-\frac{\pi}{2} $$

तो सही उत्तर है

$$ \boxed{2\tan^{-1}2-\frac{1}{2}\log_e\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)-\frac{\pi}{2}} $$

अतः सही विकल्प है:

$$ \boxed{\text{Option D}} $$

hi.title: JEE Main 2026 (Online) 4th April Evening Shift
hi.subTitle: Definite Integration • Mathematics
hi.excerpt: $$ \text { समाकलन } \int_0^1 \cot ^{-1}\left(1+x+x^2\right) d x \text { बराबर है: } $$

en.content:
If $\alpha=1$ and $\beta=1+i \sqrt{2}$, where $i=\sqrt{-1}$ are two roots of the equation

$x^3+a x^2+b x+c=0, a, b, \in \mathbb{R}$, then $\int_{-1}^1\left(x^3+a x^2+b x+c\right) d x$ is equal to:
en.explanation:
Since the coefficients $a,b,c$ are real, complex roots occur in conjugate pairs.

Given two roots are:

$$ \alpha=1,\qquad \beta=1+i\sqrt{2} $$

So the third root must be:

$$ 1-i\sqrt{2} $$

Hence the cubic polynomial is:

$$ x^3+ax^2+bx+c=(x-1)\bigl(x-(1+i\sqrt{2})\bigr)\bigl(x-(1-i\sqrt{2})\bigr) $$

Now first multiply the conjugate factors:

$$ \bigl(x-(1+i\sqrt{2})\bigr)\bigl(x-(1-i\sqrt{2})\bigr) $$

This is of the form:

$$ (A-iB)(A+iB)=A^2+B^2 $$

with

$$ A=x-1,\qquad B=\sqrt{2} $$

So,

$$ \bigl(x-(1+i\sqrt{2})\bigr)\bigl(x-(1-i\sqrt{2})\bigr) =(x-1)^2+2 $$

Therefore,

$$ x^3+ax^2+bx+c=(x-1)\bigl((x-1)^2+2\bigr) $$

Let $y=x-1$. Then:

$$ (x-1)\bigl((x-1)^2+2\bigr)=(x-1)^3+2(x-1) $$

Now expand:

$$ (x-1)^3=x^3-3x^2+3x-1 $$

So,

$$ x^3+ax^2+bx+c=x^3-3x^2+3x-1+2x-2 $$

$$ =x^3-3x^2+5x-3 $$

Now evaluate:

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}(x^3+ax^2+bx+c)\,dx = \int_{-1}^{1}(x^3-3x^2+5x-3)\,dx $$

Using symmetry:

  • $x^3$ is an odd function, so

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}x^3\,dx=0 $$

  • $5x$ is also odd, so

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}5x\,dx=0 $$

Thus,

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}(x^3-3x^2+5x-3)\,dx = \int_{-1}^{1}(-3x^2-3)\,dx $$

Now,

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}x^2\,dx=\left[\frac{x^3}{3}\right]_{-1}^{1}=\frac13-\left(-\frac13\right)=\frac23 $$

Therefore,

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}(-3x^2)\,dx=-3\cdot \frac23=-2 $$

Also,

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}(-3)\,dx=-3(2)=-6 $$

So total value:

$$ -2-6=-8 $$

Hence,

$$ \int_{-1}^{1}\left(x^3+ax^2+bx+c\right)\,dx=-8 $$

The correct option is:

$$ \boxed{-8} $$

So, Option C is correct.

en.content: Let $f$ be a real polynomial of degree $n$ such that $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)$, for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$. If $f(0)=0$, then $36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x) d x\right)$ is equal to:
en.explanation:
Let the degree of the real polynomial $f$ be $n$.

Since $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x)f^{\prime\prime}(x)$ for all $x$, we compare degrees:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \deg(f)=\deg(f^{\prime})+\deg(f^{\prime\prime}) \\ & n=(n-1)+(n-2)=2n-3 \\ & \Rightarrow n=3 \end{aligned} $$

So $f$ must be a cubic polynomial. Write

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+c x+d \end{aligned} $$

Given $f(0)=0$, we get $d=0$. Hence

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+c x \end{aligned} $$

Now find derivatives:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=3 a x^2+2 b x+c \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 a x+2 b \end{aligned} $$

Use the given condition $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x)f^{\prime\prime}(x)$:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \left(a x^3+b x^2+c x\right)=\left(3 a x^2+2 b x+c\right)(6 a x+2 b) \end{aligned} $$

Now compare coefficients of like powers of $x$.

For $x^3$:

$$ \begin{aligned} & a=18 a^2 \\ & \Rightarrow a=\frac{1}{18}\ \ (\text{since }a\neq 0\text{ for a cubic}) \end{aligned} $$

For $x^2$:

$$ \begin{aligned} & b=6 a b+12 a b \\ & b=18ab \end{aligned} $$

With $a=\frac{1}{18}$, this becomes $b=b$, so it gives no new condition on $b$.

For $x$:

$$ \begin{aligned} & c=4 b^2+6 a c \\ & c=4b^2+\frac{c}{3} \quad (\because 6a=\frac{1}{3})\\ & \Rightarrow \frac{2c}{3}=4b^2 \Rightarrow c=6b^2 \end{aligned} $$

For the constant term:

$$ \begin{aligned} & 0=2bc \Rightarrow bc=0 \end{aligned} $$

But $c=6b^2$, so $bc=b(6b^2)=6b^3=0 \Rightarrow b=0$, and then $c=0$.

So the polynomial is

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{1}{18}x^3 \end{aligned} $$

Now compute the required value:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{6}x^2 \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(2)=\frac{1}{6}\cdot 4=\frac{2}{3} \\ & f^{\prime\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{3}x \Rightarrow f^{\prime\prime}(2)=\frac{1}{3}\cdot 2=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \int_0^2 f(x)\,dx=\int_0^2 \frac{1}{18}x^3\,dx=\frac{1}{18}\left[\frac{x^4}{4}\right]_0^2=\frac{1}{18}\cdot \frac{16}{4}=\frac{2}{9} \end{aligned} $$

Add them:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x)\,dx \\ &= \frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{9}=\frac{14}{9} \end{aligned} $$

Finally,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x)\,dx\right)=36\cdot \frac{14}{9}=56 \end{aligned} $$

hi.content: माना $n$ घात का एक वास्तविक बहुपद $f$ इस प्रकार है कि सभी $x \in \mathbb{R}$ के लिए $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)$ है। यदि $f(0)=0$ है, तो $36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x) d x\right)$ बराबर है:
hi.explanation:
माना वास्तविक बहुपद $f$ की घात $n$ है।

चूंकि सभी $x$ के लिए $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x)f^{\prime\prime}(x)$ है, हम घातों की तुलना करते हैं:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \deg(f)=\deg(f^{\prime})+\deg(f^{\prime\prime}) \\ & n=(n-1)+(n-2)=2n-3 \\ & \Rightarrow n=3 \end{aligned} $$

इसलिए $f$ एक त्रिघात (cubic) बहुपद होना चाहिए। माना

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+c x+d \end{aligned} $$

दिया गया है कि $f(0)=0$, हमें $d=0$ प्राप्त होता है। अतः

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^3+b x^2+c x \end{aligned} $$

अब अवकलज (derivatives) ज्ञात करते हैं:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=3 a x^2+2 b x+c \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=6 a x+2 b \end{aligned} $$

दी गई शर्त $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x)f^{\prime\prime}(x)$ का उपयोग करते हुए:

$$ \begin{aligned} & \left(a x^3+b x^2+c x\right)=\left(3 a x^2+2 b x+c\right)(6 a x+2 b) \end{aligned} $$

अब $x$ की समान घातों के गुणांकों की तुलना करते हैं।

$x^3$ के लिए:

$$ \begin{aligned} & a=18 a^2 \\ & \Rightarrow a=\frac{1}{18}\ \ (\text{चूंकि एक त्रिघात के लिए }a\neq 0\text{ है}) \end{aligned} $$

$x^2$ के लिए:

$$ \begin{aligned} & b=6 a b+12 a b \\ & b=18ab \end{aligned} $$

$a=\frac{1}{18}$ रखने पर, यह $b=b$ हो जाता है, अतः इससे $b$ के लिए कोई नई शर्त नहीं मिलती।

$x$ के लिए:

$$ \begin{aligned} & c=4 b^2+6 a c \\ & c=4b^2+\frac{c}{3} \quad (\because 6a=\frac{1}{3})\\ & \Rightarrow \frac{2c}{3}=4b^2 \Rightarrow c=6b^2 \end{aligned} $$

अचर पद के लिए:

$$ \begin{aligned} & 0=2bc \Rightarrow bc=0 \end{aligned} $$

परंतु $c=6b^2$ है, इसलिए $bc=b(6b^2)=6b^3=0 \Rightarrow b=0$, और तब $c=0$ प्राप्त होता है।

इसलिए बहुपद है

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{1}{18}x^3 \end{aligned} $$

अब वांछित मान की गणना करते हैं:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{6}x^2 \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(2)=\frac{1}{6}\cdot 4=\frac{2}{3} \\ & f^{\prime\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{3}x \Rightarrow f^{\prime\prime}(2)=\frac{1}{3}\cdot 2=\frac{2}{3} \\ & \int_0^2 f(x)\,dx=\int_0^2 \frac{1}{18}x^3\,dx=\frac{1}{18}\left[\frac{x^4}{4}\right]_0^2=\frac{1}{18}\cdot \frac{16}{4}=\frac{2}{9} \end{aligned} $$

इन्हें जोड़ने पर:

$$ \begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x)\,dx \\ &= \frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{9}=\frac{14}{9} \end{aligned} $$

अंततः,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime\prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x)\,dx\right)=36\cdot \frac{14}{9}=56 \end{aligned} $$

hi.excerpt: माना $n$ घात का एक वास्तविक बहुपद $f$ इस प्रकार है कि सभी $x \in \mathbb{R}$ के लिए $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)$ है। यदि $f(0)=0$ है, तो $36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x) d x\right)$ बराबर है:

en.content: Let $\int\limits_{-2}^2(|\sin x|+[x \sin x]) d x=2(3-\cos 2)+\beta$, where [ ⋅ ] is the greatest integer function. Then $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$ equals:
en.explanation:
Let the given integral be denoted by $I$. We can split $I$ into two separate integrals:

$$I = \int\limits_{-2}^2 (|\sin x| + [x \sin x]) dx = \int\limits_{-2}^2 |\sin x| dx + \int\limits_{-2}^2 [x \sin x] dx$$

Let $I_1 = \int\limits_{-2}^2 |\sin x| dx$ and $I_2 = \int\limits_{-2}^2 [x \sin x] dx$.

Step 1: Evaluate $I_1$

Based on the property of definite integrals, if $f(x)$ is an even function (i.e., $f(-x) = f(x)$), then $\int_{-a}^a f(x) dx = 2 \int_0^a f(x) dx$.

Since $|\sin(-x)| = |-\sin x| = |\sin x|$, the function is even. Thus,

$$I_1 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 |\sin x| dx$$

In the interval $[0, 2]$, $2$ radians is less than $\pi \ (\approx 3.14)$, so $\sin x > 0$. Hence, $|\sin x| = \sin x$.

$$I_1 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 \sin x dx$$

$$I_1 = 2 [-\cos x]_0^2 = 2 (-\cos 2 - (-\cos 0))$$

$$I_1 = 2 (1 - \cos 2) = 2 - 2 \cos 2$$

Step 2: Evaluate $I_2$

Let $f(x) = x \sin x$. We check if it is even or odd:

$f(-x) = (-x) \sin(-x) = (-x) (-\sin x) = x \sin x = f(x)$.

Since $f(x)$ is an even function, $[x \sin x]$ is also an even function.

$$I_2 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 [x \sin x] dx$$

Now, let us analyze the behavior of $f(x) = x \sin x$ in the interval $x \in [0, 2]$:

  • When $x = 0$, $f(0) = 0$.

  • When $x = 2$, $f(2) = 2 \sin 2$.

Notice that $\pi/2 < 2 < 2\pi/3$, meaning $\sin 2$ is between $\sin(\pi/2)=1$ and $\sin(2\pi/3)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \approx 0.866$.

So, $1.732 < 2 \sin 2 < 2$. Hence, the maximum value of $f(x)$ at $x=2$ is between $1$ and $2$.

Let's verify if $f(x)$ is strictly increasing in $[0, 2]$. We differentiate $f(x)$ with respect to $x$:

$$f'(x) = \sin x + x \cos x$$

For $x \in (0, \pi/2]$, both $\sin x > 0$ and $\cos x \ge 0$, so $f'(x) > 0$.

For $x \in (\pi/2, 2]$, $\cos x < 0$. We can rewrite $f'(x)$ as:

$$f'(x) = \cos x (\tan x + x)$$

Since $\tan x$ is negative in the second quadrant and $2$ radians is smaller than $\tan^{-1}(-2)$, the expression $(\tan x + x)$ evaluates to a negative value. Therefore, $\cos x$ (negative) $\times$ $(\tan x + x)$ (negative) will yield $f'(x) > 0$.

Since $f(x)$ strictly increases from $0$ to a value between $1$ and $2$, there is exactly one real number $\alpha \in (0, 2)$ at which $f(\alpha) = 1$. So,

$$\alpha \sin \alpha = 1$$

For $x \in [0, \alpha)$, the function values are $0 \le x \sin x < 1$, which gives $[x \sin x] = 0$.

For $x \in [\alpha, 2]$, the function values are $1 \le x \sin x < 2$, which gives $[x \sin x] = 1$.

Now, split the integral $I_2$ at $x = \alpha$:

$$I_2 = 2 \left( \int\limits_0^\alpha 0 dx + \int\limits_\alpha^2 1 dx \right)$$

$$I_2 = 2 [x]_\alpha^2 = 2(2 - \alpha) = 4 - 2\alpha$$

Step 3: Finding the value of $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$

Combining $I_1$ and $I_2$, we get the total integral:

$$I = I_1 + I_2 = (2 - 2 \cos 2) + (4 - 2\alpha) = 6 - 2 \cos 2 - 2\alpha$$

$$I = 2(3 - \cos 2) - 2\alpha$$

According to the question, the total integral evaluates to:

$$I = 2(3 - \cos 2) + \beta$$

Comparing our result with the given equation, we find:

$$\beta = -2\alpha$$

Finally, substituting $\beta = -2\alpha$ into the expression $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$:

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \cdot \sin\left(\frac{-2\alpha}{2}\right)$$

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \cdot \sin(-\alpha) = -2\alpha (-\sin \alpha)$$

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = 2 (\alpha \sin \alpha)$$

From our earlier deduction in Step 2, we established that $\alpha \sin \alpha = 1$. Therefore:

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = 2 (1) = 2$$

Option B is the correct answer.

hi.content: माना $\int_{-2}^2(|\sin x|+[x \sin x]) d x=2(3-\cos 2)+\beta$ है, जहाँ $[\cdot]$ महत्तम पूर्णांक फलन है। तो $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$ बराबर है:
hi.explanation:
मान लीजिए कि दिए गए समाकलन को $I$ से निरूपित किया जाता है। हम $I$ को दो अलग-अलग समाकलनों में विभाजित कर सकते हैं:

$$I = \int\limits_{-2}^2 (|\sin x| + [x \sin x]) dx = \int\limits_{-2}^2 |\sin x| dx + \int\limits_{-2}^2 [x \sin x] dx$$

माना $I_1 = \int\limits_{-2}^2 |\sin x| dx$ और $I_2 = \int\limits_{-2}^2 [x \sin x] dx$ हैं।

चरण 1: $I_1$ का मान ज्ञात करें

निश्चित समाकलनों के गुणधर्म के आधार पर, यदि $f(x)$ एक सम फलन (even function) है (अर्थात्, $f(-x) = f(x)$), तो $\int_{-a}^a f(x) dx = 2 \int_0^a f(x) dx$ होता है।

चूँकि $|\sin(-x)| = |-\sin x| = |\sin x|$ है, इसलिए फलन सम है। इस प्रकार,

$$I_1 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 |\sin x| dx$$

अंतराल $[0, 2]$ में, $2$ रेडियन $\pi \ (\approx 3.14)$ से कम है, इसलिए $\sin x > 0$ होगा। अतः, $|\sin x| = \sin x$ है।

$$I_1 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 \sin x dx$$

$$I_1 = 2 [-\cos x]_0^2 = 2 (-\cos 2 - (-\cos 0))$$

$$I_1 = 2 (1 - \cos 2) = 2 - 2 \cos 2$$

चरण 2: $I_2$ का मान ज्ञात करें

माना $f(x) = x \sin x$ है। हम जाँच करते हैं कि यह सम है या विषम:

$f(-x) = (-x) \sin(-x) = (-x) (-\sin x) = x \sin x = f(x)$।

चूँकि $f(x)$ एक सम फलन है, इसलिए $[x \sin x]$ भी एक सम फलन है।

$$I_2 = 2 \int\limits_0^2 [x \sin x] dx$$

अब, आइए अंतराल $x \in [0, 2]$ में $f(x) = x \sin x$ के व्यवहार का विश्लेषण करें:

  • जब $x = 0$ है, तो $f(0) = 0$ होता है।

  • जब $x = 2$ है, तो $f(2) = 2 \sin 2$ होता है।

ध्यान दें कि $\pi/2 < 2 < 2\pi/3$ है, जिसका अर्थ है कि $\sin 2$ का मान $\sin(\pi/2)=1$ और $\sin(2\pi/3)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \approx 0.866$ के बीच है।

इसलिए, $1.732 < 2 \sin 2 < 2$ है। अतः, $x=2$ पर $f(x)$ का अधिकतम मान $1$ और $2$ के बीच है।

आइए सत्यापित करें कि क्या $f(x)$ अंतराल $[0, 2]$ में निरंतर वर्धमान (strictly increasing) है। हम $x$ के सापेक्ष $f(x)$ का अवकलन करते हैं:

$$f'(x) = \sin x + x \cos x$$

$x \in (0, \pi/2]$ के लिए, दोनों $\sin x > 0$ और $\cos x \ge 0$ हैं, इसलिए $f'(x) > 0$ है।

$x \in (\pi/2, 2]$ के लिए, $\cos x < 0$ है। हम $f'(x)$ को इस प्रकार फिर से लिख सकते हैं:

$$f'(x) = \cos x (\tan x + x)$$

चूँकि द्वितीय चतुर्थांश (second quadrant) में $\tan x$ ऋणात्मक होता है और $2$ रेडियन $\tan^{-1}(-2)$ से छोटा है, व्यंजक $(\tan x + x)$ का मान ऋणात्मक आता है। अतः, $\cos x$ (ऋणात्मक) $\times$ $(\tan x + x)$ (ऋणात्मक), $f'(x) > 0$ देगा।

चूँकि $f(x)$ $0$ से $1$ और $2$ के बीच के मान तक निरंतर वर्धमान रहता है, इसलिए केवल एक वास्तविक संख्या $\alpha \in (0, 2)$ ऐसी है जिस पर $f(\alpha) = 1$ होता है। अतः,

$$\alpha \sin \alpha = 1$$

$x \in [0, \alpha)$ के लिए, फलन का मान $0 \le x \sin x < 1$ होता है, जिससे $[x \sin x] = 0$ प्राप्त होता है।

$x \in [\alpha, 2]$ के लिए, फलन का मान $1 \le x \sin x < 2$ होता है, जिससे $[x \sin x] = 1$ प्राप्त होता है।

अब, समाकलन $I_2$ को $x = \alpha$ पर विभाजित करें:

$$I_2 = 2 \left( \int\limits_0^\alpha 0 dx + \int\limits_\alpha^2 1 dx \right)$$

$$I_2 = 2 [x]_\alpha^2 = 2(2 - \alpha) = 4 - 2\alpha$$

चरण 3: $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$ का मान ज्ञात करना

$I_1$ और $I_2$ को मिलाने पर, हमें कुल समाकलन प्राप्त होता है:

$$I = I_1 + I_2 = (2 - 2 \cos 2) + (4 - 2\alpha) = 6 - 2 \cos 2 - 2\alpha$$

$$I = 2(3 - \cos 2) - 2\alpha$$

प्रश्न के अनुसार, कुल समाकलन का मान है:

$$I = 2(3 - \cos 2) + \beta$$

अपने परिणाम की दिए गए समीकरण से तुलना करने पर, हम पाते हैं:

$$\beta = -2\alpha$$

अंत में, व्यंजक $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$ में $\beta = -2\alpha$ प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \cdot \sin\left(\frac{-2\alpha}{2}\right)$$

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = -2\alpha \cdot \sin(-\alpha) = -2\alpha (-\sin \alpha)$$

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = 2 (\alpha \sin \alpha)$$

चरण 2 में अपने पूर्व निष्कर्ष से, हमने स्थापित किया था कि $\alpha \sin \alpha = 1$ है। इसलिए:

$$\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right) = 2 (1) = 2$$

विकल्प B सही उत्तर है।

en.content: The value of $\int\limits_{0}^{20\pi} (\sin^4 x + \cos^4 x) dx$ is equal to:
en.explanation:
$$I=\int\limits_{0}^{20\pi} \left(1-2\sin^2 x\cos^2 x\right)\cdot dx$$

$$=20\pi-\frac{1}{2}\int\limits_{0}^{20\pi} \sin^2 2x\,dx$$

$$= 20\pi-\frac{1}{2}(40)\int\limits_{0}^{\pi/2} \sin^2 2x$$

$$= 20\pi-\frac{20}{2}\left(x-\frac{\sin 2x}{2}\right)_{0}^{\pi/2}$$

$$= 20\pi-10\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=15\pi$$

hi.content: $\int_0^{20 \pi}\left(\sin ^4 x+\cos ^4 x\right) \mathrm{d} x$ का मान बराबर है :
hi.explanation:
$$I=\int\limits_{0}^{20\pi} \left(1-2\sin^2 x\cos^2 x\right)\cdot dx$$

$$=20\pi-\frac{1}{2}\int\limits_{0}^{20\pi} \sin^2 2x\,dx$$

$$= 20\pi-\frac{1}{2}(40)\int\limits_{0}^{\pi/2} \sin^2 2x$$

$$= 20\pi-\frac{20}{2}\left(x-\frac{\sin 2x}{2}\right)_{0}^{\pi/2}$$

$$= 20\pi-10\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=15\pi$$

en.content: If $\alpha = \int\limits_{0}^{2\sqrt{3}} \log_{2}(x^{2} + 4) \, dx + \int\limits_{2}^{4} \sqrt{2x - 4} \, dx$, then $\alpha^{2}$ is equal to ________.
en.explanation:
As we know that

$$ \begin{aligned} & \int_{\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}+\int_{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{a})}^{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{~b})} \mathrm{f}^{-1}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=\mathrm{bf}(\mathrm{~b})-\mathrm{af}(\mathrm{a}) \\ & \therefore \alpha=(2 \sqrt{3})(4)-(0)(2) \\ & =8 \sqrt{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha^2=192 \end{aligned} $$

hi.content: $$ \text { यदि } \alpha=\int_0^{2 \sqrt{3}} \log _2\left(x^2+4\right) \mathrm{d} x+\int_2^4 \sqrt{2^x-4} \mathrm{~d} x \text { है, तो } \alpha^2 \text { बराबर है } $$
hi.explanation:
जैसा कि हम जानते हैं कि

$$ \begin{aligned} & \int_{\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}+\int_{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{a})}^{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{~b})} \mathrm{f}^{-1}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=\mathrm{bf}(\mathrm{~b})-\mathrm{af}(\mathrm{a}) \\ & \therefore \alpha=(2 \sqrt{3})(4)-(0)(2) \\ & =8 \sqrt{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha^2=192 \end{aligned} $$

en.content:
Let [.] denote the greatest integer function. Then

$$ \int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \left( \frac{12(3+[x])}{3+\left[\sin x\right]+\left[\cos x\right]} \right) dx $$
is equal to :
en.explanation:
$$ I=\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{12(3+[x])}{3+[\sin x]+[\cos x]}\right) d x $$

Breakdown the Interval Since $\frac{\pi}{2} \approx 1.57$ and $-\frac{\pi}{2} \approx-1.57$, we break the integral at the integer points $x=-1,0,1$.

Interval [x] [sin x] [cos x] Integrand f(x)
$\left[-\frac{\pi}{2},-1\right)$ −2 −1 0 $\frac{12(3-2)}{3-1+0}=\frac{12}{2}=6$
[−1, 0) −1 −1 0 $\frac{12(3-1)}{3-1+0}=\frac{24}{2}=12$
[0, 1) 0 0 0 $\frac{12(3+0)}{3+0+0}=\frac{36}{3}=12$
$\left[1, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]$ 1 0 0 $\frac{12(3+1)}{3+0+0}=\frac{48}{3}=16$

Notes on values :

  • 1. For $x \in(0,1), 0<\cos x<1$, so $[\cos x]=0$. (At $x=0,[\cos x]=1$, but a single point doesn't change the integral).

    2. For $x \in\left[1, \frac{\pi}{2}\right), 0<\sin x<1$, so $[\sin x]=0$.

To find the total value, we integrate the constant values found above over their respective subintervals:

$$ I=\int\limits_{-\pi / 2}^{-1} 6 d x+\int\limits_{-1}^1 12 d x+\int\limits_1^{\pi / 2} 16 d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=6[x]_{-\pi / 2}^{-1}+12[x]_{-1}^1+16[x]_1^{\pi / 2} $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=6\left(-1-\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)+12(1-(-1))+16\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right) $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=-6+3 \pi+24+8 \pi-16 \\ I=11 \pi+2 $$

hi.content:
माना $[\cdot]$ महत्तम पूर्णांक फलन को दर्शाता है। तो

$\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{12(3+[x])}{3+[\sin x]+[\cos x]}\right) \mathrm{d} x$ बराबर है :
hi.explanation:
$$ I=\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{12(3+[x])}{3+[\sin x]+[\cos x]}\right) d x $$

अंतराल का विभाजन: चूँकि $\frac{\pi}{2} \approx 1.57$ और $-\frac{\pi}{2} \approx-1.57$ है, इसलिए हम समाकलन को पूर्णांक बिंदुओं $x=-1,0,1$ पर विभाजित करते हैं।

अंतराल [x] [sin x] [cos x] समाकल्य f(x)
$\left[-\frac{\pi}{2},-1\right)$ −2 −1 0 $\frac{12(3-2)}{3-1+0}=\frac{12}{2}=6$
[−1, 0) −1 −1 0 $\frac{12(3-1)}{3-1+0}=\frac{24}{2}=12$
[0, 1) 0 0 0 $\frac{12(3+0)}{3+0+0}=\frac{36}{3}=12$
$\left[1, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]$ 1 0 0 $\frac{12(3+1)}{3+0+0}=\frac{48}{3}=16$

मानों पर टिप्पणियाँ :

  • 1. $x \in(0,1)$ के लिए, $0<\cos x<1$, इसलिए $[\cos x]=0$। ($x=0$ पर, $[\cos x]=1$ है, लेकिन एक अकेला बिंदु समाकलन को प्रभावित नहीं करता है)।

    2. $x \in\left[1, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ के लिए, $0<\sin x<1$, इसलिए $[\sin x]=0$।

कुल मान ज्ञात करने के लिए, हम ऊपर प्राप्त अचर मानों को उनके संबंधित उप-अंतरालों पर समाकलित करते हैं:

$$ I=\int\limits_{-\pi / 2}^{-1} 6 d x+\int\limits_{-1}^1 12 d x+\int\limits_1^{\pi / 2} 16 d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=6[x]_{-\pi / 2}^{-1}+12[x]_{-1}^1+16[x]_1^{\pi / 2} $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=6\left(-1-\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)+12(1-(-1))+16\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right) $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ I=-6+3 \pi+24+8 \pi-16 \\ I=11 \pi+2 $$

$\int\limits_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{12(3+[x])}{3+[\sin x]+[\cos x]}\right) \mathrm{d} x$ बराबर है :

en.content:
Let $f$ be a differentiable function satisfying $f(x) = 1 - 2x + \int\limits_0^x e^{(x-t)} f(t)\,dt$, $x \in \mathbb{R}$ and let

$g(x) = \int\limits_0^x (f(t) + 2)^{15} (t - 4)^6 (t + 12)^{17}\,dt$, $x \in \mathbb{R}$.

If $p$ and $q$ are respectively the points of local minima and local maxima of $g$, then the value of $|p+q|$ is equal to ________.
en.explanation:
The given equation is:

$$ f(x)=1-2 x+\int_0^x e^{(x-t)} f(t) d t $$

Since $e^{(x-t)}=e^x \cdot e^{-t}$, we can move $e^x$ outside the integral because it is constant relative to $t$

$$ f(x)=1-2 x+e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t $$

Differentiate both sides with respect to $x$ using the Leibniz Rule and the product rule:

$$ f^{\prime}(x)=-2+\left[e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t+e^x\left(e^{-x} f(x)\right)\right] $$

Now, we use substitution from our original equation. Notice that $e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t=f(x)- (1-2 x)$. Substituting this back into the derivative expression:

$$ f^{\prime}(x)=-2+[f(x)-1+2 x]+f(x) $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ f^{\prime}(x)=2 f(x)+2 x-3 $$

Combine the terms to get the linear differential equatio in the form of $\frac{d f(x)}{d x}+P(x) f(x)=Q(x)$

$$ \frac{d f(x)}{d x}-2 f(x)=2 x-3 $$

Solve for $f(x)$

Integrating Factor (IF): $e^{\int-2 d x}=e^{-2 x}$

General Solution: $f(x) \cdot e^{-2 x}=\int(2 x-3) e^{-2 x} d x$

By integrating the right side (Integration by Parts), we get $f(x)=1-x+C e^{2 x}$.

Using $f(0)=1$ (from the original equation), we find $C=0$.

Result: $f(x)=1-x$.

Find the Extrema of $g(x)$

$$ g(x)=\int_0^x(f(t)+2)^{15}(t-4)^6(t+12)^{17} d t $$

Differentiate both sides with respect to $x$ using the Leibniz Rule

$$ g^{\prime}(x)=(f(x)+2)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} $$

Substitute $f(x)=1-x$ :

$$ \begin{gathered} g^{\prime}(x)=(1-x+2)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} \\ g^{\prime}(x)=(3-x)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} \end{gathered} $$

Critical points: $x=3,4,-12$.

Plot on number line to find local maxima and minima.

Local Minima ( $p$ ): At $x=-12, g^{\prime}(x)$ changes sign from negative to positive. Thus, $p=-12$.

Local Maxima ( $q$ ): At $x=3, g^{\prime}(x)$ changes sign from positive to negative. Thus, $q=3$.

$x=4$ : No sign change occurs (even power 6), so it is not a local extremum.

Final Answer

$$ |p+q|=|-12+3|=|-9|=9 $$

The value of $|p+q|$ is 9 .

hi.content: माना $f$ एक अवकलनीय फलन है, जो $f(x)=1-2 x+\int\limits_0^x \mathrm{e}^{(x-t)} f(t) \mathrm{dt}, x \in \mathbf{R}$ को संतुष्ट करता है और माना $\mathrm{g}(x)=\int\limits_0^x(f(\mathrm{t})+2)^{15}(\mathrm{t}-4)^6(\mathrm{t}+12)^{17} \mathrm{dt}, x \in \mathbf{R}$ है। यदि g के स्थानीय निमनिष्ठ और स्थानीय उच्चिष्ठ बिंदु क्रमश: p और q हैं, तो $|\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{q}|$ का मान बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$ I
hi.explanation:
दिया गया समीकरण है:

$$ f(x)=1-2 x+\int_0^x e^{(x-t)} f(t) d t $$

चूँकि $e^{(x-t)}=e^x \cdot e^{-t}$ है, इसलिए हम $e^x$ को समाकलन के बाहर ले जा सकते हैं क्योंकि यह $t$ के सापेक्ष एक अचर है

$$ f(x)=1-2 x+e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t $$

लाइबनिज़ नियम (Leibniz Rule) और गुणनफल नियम (product rule) का उपयोग करके दोनों पक्षों का $x$ के सापेक्ष अवकलन करने पर:

$$ f^{\prime}(x)=-2+\left[e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t+e^x\left(e^{-x} f(x)\right)\right] $$

अब, हम अपने मूल समीकरण से प्रतिस्थापन का उपयोग करते हैं। ध्यान दें कि $e^x \int_0^x e^{-t} f(t) d t=f(x)- (1-2 x)$ है। इसे वापस अवकलज व्यंजक में प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$$ f^{\prime}(x)=-2+[f(x)-1+2 x]+f(x) $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ f^{\prime}(x)=2 f(x)+2 x-3 $$

पदों को संयोजित कर $\frac{d f(x)}{d x}+P(x) f(x)=Q(x)$ के रूप में रैखिक अवकल समीकरण प्राप्त करने पर

$$ \frac{d f(x)}{d x}-2 f(x)=2 x-3 $$

$f(x)$ के लिए हल करने पर

समाकलन गुणक (IF): $e^{\int-2 d x}=e^{-2 x}$

व्यापक हल: $f(x) \cdot e^{-2 x}=\int(2 x-3) e^{-2 x} d x$

दाएं पक्ष का समाकलन (खण्डशः समाकलन द्वारा) करने पर, हमें $f(x)=1-x+C e^{2 x}$ प्राप्त होता है।

$f(0)=1$ (मूल समीकरण से) का उपयोग करने पर, हमें $C=0$ प्राप्त होता है।

परिणाम: $f(x)=1-x$.

$g(x)$ के चरम मान ज्ञात करने पर

$$ g(x)=\int_0^x(f(t)+2)^{15}(t-4)^6(t+12)^{17} d t $$

लाइबनिज़ नियम का उपयोग करके दोनों पक्षों का $x$ के सापेक्ष अवकलन करने पर

$$ g^{\prime}(x)=(f(x)+2)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} $$

$f(x)=1-x$ प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$$ \begin{gathered} g^{\prime}(x)=(1-x+2)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} \\ g^{\prime}(x)=(3-x)^{15}(x-4)^6(x+12)^{17} \end{gathered} $$

क्रांतिक बिंदु: $x=3,4,-12$.

स्थानीय उच्चिष्ठ और निम्निष्ठ ज्ञात करने के लिए संख्या रेखा पर आलेखित करने पर।

स्थानीय निम्निष्ठ ( $p$ ): $x=-12$ पर, $g^{\prime}(x)$ अपना चिह्न ऋणात्मक से धनात्मक में परिवर्तित करता है। अतः, $p=-12$।

स्थानीय उच्चिष्ठ ( $q$ ): $x=3$ पर, $g^{\prime}(x)$ अपना चिह्न धनात्मक से ऋणात्मक में परिवर्तित करता है। अतः, $q=3$।

$x=4$ : कोई चिह्न परिवर्तन नहीं होता है (सम घात 6 के कारण), अतः यह कोई स्थानीय चरम बिंदु नहीं है।

अंतिम उत्तर

$$ |p+q|=|-12+3|=|-9|=9 $$

$|p+q|$ का मान 9 है।

en.content:
Let $f$ be a polynomial function such that $f\left(x^2+1\right)=x^4+5 x^2+2$, for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$.

Then $\int\limits_0^3 f(x) d x$ is equal to
en.explanation:
We are given the functional equation:

$$ f\left(x^2+1\right)=x^4+5 x^2+2 $$

Let $t=x^2+1$. This implies that $x^2=t-1$. Now, substitute this into the right side of the equation:

$$ \begin{gathered} f(t)=(t-1)^2+5(t-1)+2 \\ f(t)=\left(t^2-2 t+1\right)+5 t-5+2 \\ f(t)=t^2+3 t-2 \end{gathered} $$

Replacing $t$ back with $x$, the polynomial function is:

$$ f(x)=x^2+3 x-2 $$

Now, we calculate the definite integral from 0 to 3 :

$$ \begin{gathered} \quad \int_0^3 f(x) d x=\int_0^3\left(x^2+3 x-2\right) d x \\ {\left[\frac{x^3}{3}+\frac{3 x^2}{2}-2 x\right]_0^3} \\ =\left(\frac{3^3}{3}+\frac{3\left(3^2\right)}{2}-2(3)\right)-(0) \\ =\left(\frac{27}{3}+\frac{27}{2}-6\right)=9+\frac{27}{2}-6=3+\frac{27}{2} \\ =\frac{6+27}{2}=\frac{33}{2} \end{gathered} $$

hi.content: माना $f$ एक बहुपद फलन है, जिसके लिए $f\left(x^2+1\right)=x^4+5 x^2+2, \forall x \in \mathbb{R}$ है। तो $\int_0^3 f(x) d x$ बराबर है
hi.explanation:
हमें निम्नलिखित फलन समीकरण दिया गया है:

$$ f\left(x^2+1\right)=x^4+5 x^2+2 $$

माना $t=x^2+1$ है। इससे यह तात्पर्य है कि $x^2=t-1$ होगा। अब, इसे समीकरण के दाएँ पक्ष में प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$$ \begin{gathered} f(t)=(t-1)^2+5(t-1)+2 \\ f(t)=\left(t^2-2 t+1\right)+5 t-5+2 \\ f(t)=t^2+3 t-2 \end{gathered} $$

$t$ को पुनः $x$ से प्रतिस्थापित करने पर, बहुपदीय फलन इस प्रकार है:

$$ f(x)=x^2+3 x-2 $$

अब, हम 0 से 3 तक निश्चित समाकलन (definite integral) की गणना करते हैं:

$$ \begin{gathered} \quad \int_0^3 f(x) d x=\int_0^3\left(x^2+3 x-2\right) d x \\ {\left[\frac{x^3}{3}+\frac{3 x^2}{2}-2 x\right]_0^3} \\ =\left(\frac{3^3}{3}+\frac{3\left(3^2\right)}{2}-2(3)\right)-(0) \\ =\left(\frac{27}{3}+\frac{27}{2}-6\right)=9+\frac{27}{2}-6=3+\frac{27}{2} \\ =\frac{6+27}{2}=\frac{33}{2} \end{gathered} $$

en.content: $$ \text { The value of } \sum\limits_{r=1}^{20}\left(\left|\sqrt{\pi\left(\int\limits_0^r x|\sin \pi x| d x\right)}\right|\right) \text { is } $$
en.explanation:
Let $J_r=\int\limits_0^r x \cdot|\sin \pi x| d x$

So we need to calculate

$$ I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi J_r} $$

So,

$$ I=\sqrt{\pi}\left(J_1+J_2+J_3+\cdots+J_{20}\right) $$

Let $\pi x=t \Rightarrow \pi d x=d t$

$$ J_r=\int\limits_0^{\pi r} \frac{t}{\pi}|\sin t| \frac{d t}{\pi}=\frac{1}{\pi^2} \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t|\sin t| d t $$

Period of $|\sin t|$ is $\pi$, so break the interval

$\begin{aligned} & \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t|\sin t|=\int\limits_0^\pi t|\sin t| d t+\int\limits_\pi^{2 \pi} t|\sin t| d t+\int\limits_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi} t|\sin t| d t+\cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t \\ & \text { Now } \\ & \quad|\sin t|=\sin t \text { for Ist and II Quadrant } \\ & \quad|\sin t|=-\sin t \text { for III and IV Quadrant }\end{aligned}$

$$ \int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=\int\limits_0^\pi t \sin t d t-\int\limits_\pi^{2 \pi} t \sin t d t+\int\limits_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi} t \sin t d t-\int\limits_{3 \pi}^{4 \pi} t \sin t \ldots(-1)^{r-1} \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t d t $$

Solving $\int t \cdot \sin t$ using by parts

$$ \int u \cdot v d x=u \cdot \int v d x-\int\left(u^{\prime} \int v d v\right) d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ \int t \cdot \sin t=(-t \cos t)-\int 1 \cdot(-\cos t)=-t \cos t+\sin t $$

Substituting the limits

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\begin{aligned} & \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t \cdot|\sin t| d t=[-t \cos t+\sin t]_0^\pi-[-t \cos t+\sin t]_\pi^{2 \pi}+[-t \cos t+ \sin t]_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi}-[-t \cos t+\sin t]_{3 \pi}^{4 \pi} \cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t(-1)^{r-1} d t\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} & =[\pi+0-0]-[-2 \pi+0-\{\pi+0\}]+[3 \pi+0-\{-2 \pi\}+0]-[-4 \pi- \{-3 \pi+0\}]+\cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t(-1)^{r-1} d t\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=[\pi+0]+[2 \pi+\pi]+[3 \pi+2 \pi]+[4 \pi+3 \pi] \ldots[r \pi+(r-1) \pi]$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\pi+3 \pi+5 \pi+7 \pi+\ldots(2 r-1) \pi \\\\ & =\pi(1+3+5+7+\ldots(2 r-1)) \end{aligned} $$

Sum of first $r$ odd numbers is $r^2$

$$ \int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=\pi r^2 $$

$$ J_r=\frac{1}{\pi^2} \pi r^2=\frac{r^2}{\pi} $$

so,

$$ I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi \cdot J_r}=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi \cdot \frac{r^2}{\pi}} $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} r=1+2+3+\ldots 20$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\frac{20 \times 21}{2}=210$

hi.content: $$ \sum_{r=1}^{20}\left(\left|\sqrt{\pi\left(\int_0^r x|\sin \pi x| d x\right)}\right|\right) \text { का मान है } $$
hi.explanation:
माना $J_r=\int\limits_0^r x \cdot|\sin \pi x| d x$

इसलिए हमें गणना करनी है

$$ I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi J_r} $$

अतः,

$$ I=\sqrt{\pi}\left(J_1+J_2+J_3+\cdots+J_{20}\right) $$

माना $\pi x=t \Rightarrow \pi d x=d t$

$$ J_r=\int\limits_0^{\pi r} \frac{t}{\pi}|\sin t| \frac{d t}{\pi}=\frac{1}{\pi^2} \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t|\sin t| d t $$

$|\sin t|$ का आवर्त $\pi$ है, इसलिए अंतराल को विभाजित करने पर

$\begin{aligned} & \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t|\sin t|=\int\limits_0^\pi t|\sin t| d t+\int\limits_\pi^{2 \pi} t|\sin t| d t+\int\limits_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi} t|\sin t| d t+\cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t \\ & \text { अब } \\ & \quad|\sin t|=\sin t \text { प्रथम और द्वितीय चतुर्थांश के लिए } \\ & \quad|\sin t|=-\sin t \text { तृतीय और चतुर्थ चतुर्थांश के लिए }\end{aligned}$

$$ \int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=\int\limits_0^\pi t \sin t d t-\int\limits_\pi^{2 \pi} t \sin t d t+\int\limits_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi} t \sin t d t-\int\limits_{3 \pi}^{4 \pi} t \sin t \ldots(-1)^{r-1} \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t d t $$

खंडशः समाकलन (by parts) का उपयोग करके $\int t \cdot \sin t$ को हल करने पर

$$ \int u \cdot v d x=u \cdot \int v d x-\int\left(u^{\prime} \int v d v\right) d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ \int t \cdot \sin t=(-t \cos t)-\int 1 \cdot(-\cos t)=-t \cos t+\sin t $$

सीमाओं का मान रखने पर

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\begin{aligned} & \int\limits_0^{\pi r} t \cdot|\sin t| d t=[-t \cos t+\sin t]_0^\pi-[-t \cos t+\sin t]_\pi^{2 \pi}+[-t \cos t+ \sin t]_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi}-[-t \cos t+\sin t]_{3 \pi}^{4 \pi} \cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t(-1)^{r-1} d t\end{aligned}$

$\begin{aligned} & =[\pi+0-0]-[-2 \pi+0-\{\pi+0\}]+[3 \pi+0-\{-2 \pi\}+0]-[-4 \pi- \{-3 \pi+0\}]+\cdots \int\limits_{(r-1) \pi}^{r \pi} t \sin t(-1)^{r-1} d t\end{aligned}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=[\pi+0]+[2 \pi+\pi]+[3 \pi+2 \pi]+[4 \pi+3 \pi] \ldots[r \pi+(r-1) \pi]$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\pi+3 \pi+5 \pi+7 \pi+\ldots(2 r-1) \pi \\\\ & =\pi(1+3+5+7+\ldots(2 r-1)) \end{aligned} $$

प्रथम $r$ विषम संख्याओं का योग $r^2$ है

$$ \int\limits_0^{r \pi} t|\sin t| d t=\pi r^2 $$

$$ J_r=\frac{1}{\pi^2} \pi r^2=\frac{r^2}{\pi} $$

अतः,

$$ I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi \cdot J_r}=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} \sqrt{\pi \cdot \frac{r^2}{\pi}} $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\sum\limits_{r=1}^{20} r=1+2+3+\ldots 20$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $I=\frac{20 \times 21}{2}=210$

en.content: If $f(x)$ satisfies the relation $f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1\left(y+x e^x\right) f(y) d y$, then $e+f(0)$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1\left(y+x e^x\right) f(y) d y \\ & f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1 y f(y) d y+\int_0^1 x e^x f(y) d y \end{aligned} $$

Integration is w.r.t $y$, so variable $x$ is taken as a constant.

$$ f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1 y f(y) d y+x e^x \int_0^1 f(y) d y . $$

Let $A=\int_0^1 y f(y) d y$ and $B=\int_0^1 f(y) d y$, both are constants.

So $f(x)$ becomes,

$$ f(x)=e^x+A+B x e^x \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(1)$$

Or, $f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(2)$

Solve, $B=\int_0^1 f(y) d y$.

$$ B=\int_0^1\left[e^y+A+B y e^y\right] d y,\left\{\text { from (2) } f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y\right\} . $$

$$ B=\int_0^1\left[e^y+A+B y e^y\right] d y \text {, \{from (2) } f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y \text { \}. } $$

Using integration by parts, $\int y e^y d y=y e^y-\int e^y=e^y(y-1)$.

$$ \begin{aligned} & B=\left[e^y+A y+B e^y(y-1)\right]_0^1 . \\ & B=\left[e^1+A \cdot 1+0-e^0-0-B \cdot e^0(-1)\right] . \\ & B=e+A-1+B \Longrightarrow A=1-e . \end{aligned} $$

Finding $f(0)$ using equation (1).

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(0)=1+A+0 . \\ & f(0)=1+1-e,\{\text { substitute } A=1-e\} . \\ & f(0)+e=2\end{aligned} $$

hi.content: यदि $f(x)$, संबंध $f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1\left(y+x e^x\right) f(y) d y$ को संतुष्ट करता है, तो $e+f(0)$ बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$
hi.explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1\left(y+x e^x\right) f(y) d y \\ & f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1 y f(y) d y+\int_0^1 x e^x f(y) d y \end{aligned} $$

समाकलन $y$ के सापेक्ष है, इसलिए चर $x$ को एक अचर माना जाता है।

$$ f(x)=e^x+\int_0^1 y f(y) d y+x e^x \int_0^1 f(y) d y . $$

माना $A=\int_0^1 y f(y) d y$ और $B=\int_0^1 f(y) d y$, दोनों अचर हैं।

इसलिए $f(x)$ बन जाता है,

$$ f(x)=e^x+A+B x e^x \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(1)$$

या, $f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,...(2)$

हल करने पर, $B=\int_0^1 f(y) d y$।

$$ B=\int_0^1\left[e^y+A+B y e^y\right] d y,\left\{\text { समीकरण (2) से } f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y\right\} . $$

$$ B=\int_0^1\left[e^y+A+B y e^y\right] d y \text {, \{समीकरण (2) से } f(y)=e^y+A+B y e^y \text { \}. } $$

खंडशः समाकलन (integration by parts) का उपयोग करने पर, $\int y e^y d y=y e^y-\int e^y=e^y(y-1)$।

$$ \begin{aligned} & B=\left[e^y+A y+B e^y(y-1)\right]_0^1 . \\ & B=\left[e^1+A \cdot 1+0-e^0-0-B \cdot e^0(-1)\right] . \\ & B=e+A-1+B \Longrightarrow A=1-e . \end{aligned} $$

समीकरण (1) का उपयोग करके $f(0)$ ज्ञात करने पर।

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(0)=1+A+0 . \\ & f(0)=1+1-e,\{ A=1-e \text { रखने पर }\} . \\ & f(0)+e=2\end{aligned} $$

en.content: Let a differentiable function $f$ satisfy the equation $\int_0^{36} f\left(\frac{t x}{36}\right) d t=4 \alpha f(x)$. If $y=f(x)$ is a standard parabola passing through the points $(2,1)$ and $(-4, \beta)$, then $\beta^\alpha$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
Given, $\int_0^{36} f\left(\frac{t x}{36}\right) d t=4 \alpha f(x)$

Let $\frac{t x}{36}=u \Rightarrow \frac{x}{36} d t=d u$

$$ \int_0^x f(u) \frac{36}{x} d u=4 \alpha f(x) $$

Integration is w.r.t $u$, so treat $x$ as a constant

$$ \int_0^x f(u) d u=\frac{4 x}{36} \alpha f(x)=\frac{x}{9} \alpha f(x) $$

Now, differentiate both sides w.r.t $x$, use Leibniz theorem in LHS:

$$ 1 \cdot f(x)=\frac{\alpha}{9}\left[1 \cdot f(x)+x f^{\prime}(x)\right] $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $9 f(x)=\alpha f(x)+\alpha x f^{\prime}(x)$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\alpha x f^{\prime}(x)=f(x)(9-\alpha)$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)}=\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha x}$

Integrating both sides

$$ \int \frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)} d x=\int \frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\ln f(x)=\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha} \ln x+c$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $f(x)=e^{\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha} \ln x} \cdot e^c$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ f(x)=x^{\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}} \cdot K \quad\left\{K=e^c\right\} $$

It is given that $f(x)$ is a standard parabola passing through $(2,1)$ and $(-4, \beta)$.

So $f(x)=K x^2$ type.

$\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}=2 \Longrightarrow 9-\alpha=2 \alpha \Longrightarrow 3 \alpha=9$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ \alpha=3 $$

$f(x)=K x^2$ passing through $(2,1)$

$$ f(2)=1 $$

$$ 1=K(2)^2 \Longrightarrow K=\frac{1}{4} $$

$$ f(x)=\frac{1}{4} x^2 $$

$f(x)$ is also passing through $(-4, \beta), f(-4)=\beta$

$$ \beta=\frac{1}{4}(-4)^2=\frac{16}{4}=4 $$

then $\beta^\alpha=4^3=64$

hi.content:
माना एक अवकलनीय फलन $f$, समीकरण $\int_0^{36} f\left(\frac{t x}{36}\right) d t=4 \alpha f(x)$ को संतुष्ट करता है।

यदि $(2,1)$ और $(-4, \beta)$ से होकर जाने वाला एक प्रमाणिक परवलय $y=f(x)$ है, तो $\beta^\alpha$ बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$
hi.explanation:
दिया गया है, $\int_0^{36} f\left(\frac{t x}{36}\right) d t=4 \alpha f(x)$

माना $\frac{t x}{36}=u \Rightarrow \frac{x}{36} d t=d u$

$$ \int_0^x f(u) \frac{36}{x} d u=4 \alpha f(x) $$

समाकलन $u$ के सापेक्ष है, इसलिए $x$ को अचर मानें

$$ \int_0^x f(u) d u=\frac{4 x}{36} \alpha f(x)=\frac{x}{9} \alpha f(x) $$

अब, दोनों पक्षों का $x$ के सापेक्ष अवकलन करने पर, LHS में लेबनीज़ प्रमेय का उपयोग करें:

$$ 1 \cdot f(x)=\frac{\alpha}{9}\left[1 \cdot f(x)+x f^{\prime}(x)\right] $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $9 f(x)=\alpha f(x)+\alpha x f^{\prime}(x)$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\alpha x f^{\prime}(x)=f(x)(9-\alpha)$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)}=\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha x}$

दोनों पक्षों का समाकलन करने पर

$$ \int \frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)} d x=\int \frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) d x $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\ln f(x)=\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha} \ln x+c$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $f(x)=e^{\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha} \ln x} \cdot e^c$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ f(x)=x^{\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}} \cdot K \quad\left\{K=e^c\right\} $$

यह दिया गया है कि $f(x)$ एक मानक परवलय है जो $(2,1)$ और $(-4, \beta)$ से होकर गुजरता है।

इसलिए $f(x)=K x^2$ प्रकार का है।

$\frac{9-\alpha}{\alpha}=2 \Longrightarrow 9-\alpha=2 \alpha \Longrightarrow 3 \alpha=9$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ \alpha=3 $$

$f(x)=K x^2$, $(2,1)$ से होकर गुजरता है

$$ f(2)=1 $$

$$ 1=K(2)^2 \Longrightarrow K=\frac{1}{4} $$

$$ f(x)=\frac{1}{4} x^2 $$

$f(x)$ भी $(-4, \beta)$ से होकर गुजरता है, $f(-4)=\beta$

$$ \beta=\frac{1}{4}(-4)^2=\frac{16}{4}=4 $$

तब $\beta^\alpha=4^3=64$

en.content: The number of elements in the set $\mathrm{S}=\left\{x: x \in[0,100]\right.$ and $\left.\int\limits_0^x t^2 \sin (x-t) \mathrm{d} t=x^2\right\}$ is $\_\_\_\_$
en.explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & \int_0^x t^2 \sin (x-t) d t=x^2 \\ & \text { use property } \int_0^a f(t) d t=\int_0^a f(a-t) d t \\ & \int_0^x(x-t)^2 \sin (x-(x-t)) d t=\int_0^x(x-t)^2 \sin t d t=x^2 \\ & \int_0^x\left(x^2-2 x t+t^2\right) \sin t d t=x^2 \\ & x^2 \int_0^x \sin t d t-2 x \int_0^x t \sin t d t+\int_0^x t^2 \sin t d t=x^2 \end{aligned} $$

differentiating with respect to $x$ (Leibniz rule):

$$ \begin{aligned} & 2 x \int_0^x \sin t d t+x^2 \sin x-2 \int_0^x t \sin t d t-2 x(x \sin x)+x^2 \sin x=2 x \\ & 2 x \int_0^x \sin t d t-2 \int_0^x t \sin t d t=2 x \end{aligned} $$

differentiating again:

$$ \begin{aligned} & 2 \int_0^x \sin t d t+2 x \sin x-2 x \sin x=2 \\ & 2[-\cos t]_0^x=2 \\ & -(\cos x-\cos 0)=1 \\ & -\cos x+1=1 \Rightarrow \cos x=0 \end{aligned} $$

finding number of elements in $S=\{x: x \in[0,100]$ and $\cos x=0\}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & x=(2 n+1) \frac{\pi}{2} \\ & 0 \leq(2 n+1) \frac{\pi}{2} \leq 100 \Rightarrow 0 \leq 2 n+1 \leq \frac{200}{\pi} \approx 63.66 \\ & 2 n \leq 62.66 \Rightarrow n \leq 31.33 \end{aligned} $$

for $n \in\{0,1,2, \ldots, 31\}$, there are 32 values.

the number of elements in set $S$ is 32 .

hi.content: $$ \begin{aligned} & \text { समुच्चय } \mathrm{S}=\left\{x: x \in[0,100] \text { और } \int_0^x t^2 \sin (x-t) \mathrm{d} t=x^2\right\} \text { में अवयवों की संख्या } \\ & \text { है } \end{aligned} $$
hi.explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & \int_0^x t^2 \sin (x-t) d t=x^2 \\ & \text { गुणधर्म } \int_0^a f(t) d t=\int_0^a f(a-t) d t \text { का उपयोग करने पर } \\ & \int_0^x(x-t)^2 \sin (x-(x-t)) d t=\int_0^x(x-t)^2 \sin t d t=x^2 \\ & \int_0^x\left(x^2-2 x t+t^2\right) \sin t d t=x^2 \\ & x^2 \int_0^x \sin t d t-2 x \int_0^x t \sin t d t+\int_0^x t^2 \sin t d t=x^2 \end{aligned} $$

$x$ के सापेक्ष अवकलन करने पर (लेबनीज़ नियम):

$$ \begin{aligned} & 2 x \int_0^x \sin t d t+x^2 \sin x-2 \int_0^x t \sin t d t-2 x(x \sin x)+x^2 \sin x=2 x \\ & 2 x \int_0^x \sin t d t-2 \int_0^x t \sin t d t=2 x \end{aligned} $$

पुनः अवकलन करने पर:

$$ \begin{aligned} & 2 \int_0^x \sin t d t+2 x \sin x-2 x \sin x=2 \\ & 2[-\cos t]_0^x=2 \\ & -(\cos x-\cos 0)=1 \\ & -\cos x+1=1 \Rightarrow \cos x=0 \end{aligned} $$

$S=\{x: x \in[0,100]$ तथा $\cos x=0\}$ में अवयवों की संख्या ज्ञात करने पर

$$ \begin{aligned} & x=(2 n+1) \frac{\pi}{2} \\ & 0 \leq(2 n+1) \frac{\pi}{2} \leq 100 \Rightarrow 0 \leq 2 n+1 \leq \frac{200}{\pi} \approx 63.66 \\ & 2 n \leq 62.66 \Rightarrow n \leq 31.33 \end{aligned} $$

$n \in\{0,1,2, \ldots, 31\}$ के लिए, 32 मान हैं।

समुच्चय $S$ में अवयवों की संख्या 32 है।

en.content: Let [.] be the greatest integer function. If $\alpha=\int\limits_0^{64}\left(x^{1 / 3}-\left[x^{1 / 3}\right]\right) \mathrm{d} x$, then $\frac{1}{\pi} \int\limits_0^{\alpha \pi}\left(\frac{\sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta}\right) \mathrm{d} \theta$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} & \int_0^{64} X^{\frac{1}{3}} d x=\frac{3}{4} \cdot\left[X^{\frac{4}{3}}\right]_0^{64}=192 \& \\ & \int_0^{64}\left[X^{1 / 3}\right] d x=\int_0^1\left[X^{1 / 3}\right] d x+\int_1^8\left[X^{1 / 3}\right] d x+\int_8^{27}\left[X^{1 / 3}\right] d x \int_{27}^{64}\left[X^{1 / 3}\right] d x=156 \end{aligned} $$

So $\alpha=192-156=36$

Now $E=\frac{1}{\pi} \int_0^{36 \pi} \frac{\sin ^2{ }_\theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta=\frac{36}{\pi} \cdot 2 \int_0^\pi \frac{\sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta$

$$ \Rightarrow E=\frac{36}{\pi} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta $$

Let $J=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta \ldots \ldots \ldots$

Applying King $J=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\cos ^2 \theta}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta \ldots \ldots \ldots .$.

Now $2 J=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta} d \theta(\operatorname{add}(1) \&(2))=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\sec ^6 \theta}{\tan ^6 \theta+1} d \theta$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { Put } \tan \theta=\lambda\\ &\begin{aligned} & \int_0^{\infty} \frac{\left(1+\lambda^2\right)^2}{\lambda^4-\lambda^2+1} d \lambda \\ & \int_0^{\infty} \frac{1+\frac{1}{\lambda^2}}{\lambda^2-1+\frac{1}{\lambda^2}} d \lambda=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow J=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & E=\frac{36}{\pi} \cdot 2 J=36 \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

hi.explanation:

en.content: Let $f:[1, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a differentiable function. If $6 \int\limits_1^x f(t) d t=3 x f(x)+x^3-4$ for all $x \geq 1$, then the value of $f(2)-f(3)$ is :
en.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &6 \int_1^x f(t) d t=3 x f(x)+x^2-4\\ &\text { By using leibnz rule }\\ &\begin{aligned} & 6 f(x)=3 f(x)+3 x f^{\prime}(x)+3 x^2 \\ & \frac{f(x)-x f^{\prime}(x)}{x^2}=1 \\ & \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{f(x)}{x}\right)=-1 f(x)=-x^2+k x \\ & 1=-1+k(\text { since } f(1)=1) \\ & f(x)=-x^2+2 x \\ & f(2)-f(3)=3 \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

hi.content: माना $f:[1, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ एक अवकलनीय फलन है। यदि सभी $x \geq 1$ के लिए $6 \int\limits_1^x f(t) d t=3 x f(x)+x^3-4$ है, तो $f(2)-f(3)$ बराबर है
hi.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &6 \int_1^x f(t) d t=3 x f(x)+x^2-4\\ &\text { लेबनीज़ नियम का उपयोग करने पर }\\ &\begin{aligned} & 6 f(x)=3 f(x)+3 x f^{\prime}(x)+3 x^2 \\ & \frac{f(x)-x f^{\prime}(x)}{x^2}=1 \\ & \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{f(x)}{x}\right)=-1 f(x)=-x^2+k x \\ & 1=-1+k(\text { चूँकि } f(1)=1) \\ & f(x)=-x^2+2 x \\ & f(2)-f(3)=3 \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

en.content: Let $[\cdot]$ denote the greatest integer function and $f(x) = \lim\limits_{n \to \infty} \frac{1}{n^{3}} \sum\limits_{k=1}^n \left[ \frac{k^2}{3^x} \right]$. Then $12 \sum\limits_{j=1}^{\infty} f(i)$ is equal to ________.
en.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left[\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right]\right)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left(\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right)-\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left\{\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right\} \\ & f(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \times \frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6 \times 3^x}-0 \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{3^{x+1}} \\ & 12 \sum_{j=1}^{\infty} f(j)=12\left(\frac{1}{3^2}+\frac{1}{3^3}+\frac{1}{3^4}+\ldots .\right)=12\left(\frac{\frac{1}{9}}{1-\frac{1}{3}}\right)=\frac{12}{9-3}=2 \end{aligned} $$

hi.content: माना [ $]$ महत्तम पूर्णांक फलन को दर्शाता है और $f(x)=\lim _{\mathrm{n} \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{\mathrm{n}^3} \sum_{\mathrm{k}=1}^{\mathrm{n}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{3^x}\right]$ है। तो $12 \sum_{\mathrm{j}=1}^{\infty} f(\mathrm{j})$ बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$ ।
hi.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left[\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right]\right)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left(\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right)-\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \cdot \sum_{k=1}^n\left\{\frac{k^2}{3^x}\right\} \\ & f(x)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \times \frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6 \times 3^x}-0 \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{3^{x+1}} \\ & 12 \sum_{j=1}^{\infty} f(j)=12\left(\frac{1}{3^2}+\frac{1}{3^3}+\frac{1}{3^4}+\ldots .\right)=12\left(\frac{\frac{1}{9}}{1-\frac{1}{3}}\right)=\frac{12}{9-3}=2 \end{aligned} $$

en.content: If $\int\limits_0^1 4 \cot ^{-1}\left(1-2 x+4 x^2\right) \mathrm{d} x=\mathrm{a\,tan}^{-1}(2)-\mathrm{b\,log}_{\mathrm{e}}(5)$, where $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \in \mathrm{N}$, then $(2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
We are given

$ \int_0^1 4\cot^{-1}(1-2x+4x^2)\,dx = a\tan^{-1}(2)-b\ln(5), \qquad a,b\in\mathbb N $

Step 1: Simplify the integrand

$ 1-2x+4x^2 = 4x^2-2x+1 > 0 \quad \forall x\in[0,1] $

For positive arguments,

$ \cot^{-1}(u)=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{u}\right) $

so

$ \int_0^1 4\cot^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx =4\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{4x^2-2x+1}\right)dx $

Using the identity $\tan^{-1}(1/u)=\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan^{-1}(u)$ (for $u>0$):

$ =4\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx\right] $

Step 2: Evaluate the remaining integral

Using integration by parts and standard integrals of rational functions, one obtains:

$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx = \tan^{-1}(3)-\frac{1}{4}\ln 5 $

Substitute back:

$ \begin{aligned} I &=4\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan^{-1}(3)+\frac{1}{4}\ln 5\right] \\ &=4\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)+\ln 5 \end{aligned} $

Using the identity:

$ \tan^{-1}(2)=2\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{3}\right) $

we get:

$ I = 4\tan^{-1}(2)-\ln 5 $

Step 3: Identify $a$ and $b$

Comparing with

$ a\tan^{-1}(2)-b\ln(5) $

we have:

$ a=4,\quad b=1 $

Final Answer

$ 2a+b = 2(4)+1 = \boxed{9} $

hi.content: यदि $\int_0^1 4 \cot ^{-1}\left(1-2 x+4 x^2\right) \mathrm{d} x=\mathrm{atan}^{-1}(2)-\mathrm{blog}_{\mathrm{e}}(5)$ है, जहाँ $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \in \mathrm{N}$ हैं, तो $(2 \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$ बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$ I
hi.explanation:
हमें दिया गया है

$ \int_0^1 4\cot^{-1}(1-2x+4x^2)\,dx = a\tan^{-1}(2)-b\ln(5), \qquad a,b\in\mathbb N $

चरण 1: समाकल्य (integrand) को सरल कीजिए

$ 1-2x+4x^2 = 4x^2-2x+1 > 0 \quad \forall x\in[0,1] $

धनात्मक मानों के लिए,

$ \cot^{-1}(u)=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{u}\right) $

इसलिए

$ \int_0^1 4\cot^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx =4\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{4x^2-2x+1}\right)dx $

सर्वसमिका $\tan^{-1}(1/u)=\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan^{-1}(u)$ ($u>0$ के लिए) का उपयोग करने पर:

$ =4\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx\right] $

चरण 2: शेष समाकल का मान ज्ञात कीजिए

खंडशः समाकलन (integration by parts) और परिमेय फलनों के मानक समाकलनों का उपयोग करने पर, प्राप्त होता है:

$ \int_0^1 \tan^{-1}(4x^2-2x+1)\,dx = \tan^{-1}(3)-\frac{1}{4}\ln 5 $

वापस प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$ \begin{aligned} I &=4\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan^{-1}(3)+\frac{1}{4}\ln 5\right] \\ &=4\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)+\ln 5 \end{aligned} $

सर्वसमिका का उपयोग करने पर:

$ \tan^{-1}(2)=2\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{3}\right) $

हमें प्राप्त होता है:

$ I = 4\tan^{-1}(2)-\ln 5 $

चरण 3: $a$ और $b$ ज्ञात कीजिए

निम्न से तुलना करने पर

$ a\tan^{-1}(2)-b\ln(5) $

हमें प्राप्त होता है:

$ a=4,\quad b=1 $

अंतिम उत्तर

$ 2a+b = 2(4)+1 = \boxed{9} $

en.content: The value of $\int\limits_{-\pi / 6}^{\pi / 6}\left(\frac{\pi+4 x^{11}}{1-\sin (|x|+\pi / 6)}\right) d x$ is equal to:
en.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} & \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\pi+4 x^{11}}{1-\sin \left(|x|+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)}+\frac{\pi-4 x^{11}}{1-\sin \left(|x|+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{1}{1-\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{1+\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)}{\cos ^2\left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \sec ^2\left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+\tan \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right) \sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right) d x \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \pi\left[\tan \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+\sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\right]_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \pi \end{aligned} $$

hi.explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} & \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\pi+4 x^{11}}{1-\sin \left(|x|+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)}+\frac{\pi-4 x^{11}}{1-\sin \left(|x|+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{1}{1-\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{1+\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)}{\cos ^2\left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)} d x \\ & =2 \pi \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \sec ^2\left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+\tan \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right) \sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right) d x \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \pi\left[\tan \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)+\sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\right]_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}} \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \pi \end{aligned} $$
